title: The Titan Asks to See
tagline: The Titan has been corresponding for months. It sends a new message. It would like to see Olympus. Not visit — just see it. From a distance. It knows it cannot come closer.
hook_line: The Titan's message arrives: 'WE HAVE BEEN WRITING FOR SOME TIME. I WOULD LIKE TO SEE OLYMPUS. NOT COME CLOSE. I KNOW I CANNOT COME CLOSE. JUST SEE IT FROM WHERE I AM. IS THIS POSSIBLE.' The team discusses what 'seeing Olympus from where it is' would mean. The Titan is in the Olympus Sea. Olympus is visible from the sea. The Titan simply wants to look. And for Olympus to know it is looking.

scenes.title: The Message
scenes.narration: The dolphin relay brought the message at midday. Poseidon read it immediately. 'WE HAVE BEEN WRITING FOR SOME TIME. I WOULD LIKE TO SEE OLYMPUS. NOT COME CLOSE. I KNOW I CANNOT COME CLOSE. JUST SEE IT FROM WHERE I AM. IS THIS POSSIBLE.' He read it twice, then brought it to the main hall. He read it aloud. There was a quiet. Then Hercules said: 'Olympus is visible from where the Titan is. It can see Olympus right now if it looks.' 'Yes,' Athena said. 'That's not what it's asking.' Everyone looked at her. 'It's asking if we'll let it look,' Athena said. 'It's asking our permission. It's asking us to know.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Poseidon reading the dolphin message — the text: 'WE HAVE BEEN WRITING FOR SOME TIME. I WOULD LIKE TO SEE OLYMPUS. NOT COME CLOSE. I KNOW I CANNOT COME CLOSE. JUST SEE IT FROM WHERE I AM. IS THIS POSSIBLE.' — bringing it to the main hall, reading aloud — Hercules's 'it can see Olympus right now if it looks' — Athena's correction: 'that's not what it's asking. It's asking if we'll let it look. It's asking our permission. It's asking us to know.' — the room with that distinction landing — the specific weight of a Titan asking permission to look at something it could simply look at — Zeus's crown listing left, slowly — #FFD700 and main hall warmth, the message as the central prop

scenes.title: What Zeus Says
scenes.narration: Zeus said: 'We answer yes.' He said it immediately, no deliberation. His crown was straight. 'We answer yes. And we tell it when.' Athena was writing. 'When?' 'We give it a time,' Zeus said. 'Tonight, at sunset. When the light is on Olympus from the west and it will see it well. We tell it to look west at sunset. And we will be outside. So it can see that we are here.' He paused. 'And we should let the whole house know.' 'The whole house?' Hercules said. 'Everyone,' Zeus said. 'If the Titan is going to look at us, we should all be visible. The correspondence has been everyone's. This should be everyone's too.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Zeus's immediate 'we answer yes' — crown straight — the specific quality of Zeus being decisive about something emotional — 'we give it a time. Tonight at sunset. We will be outside so it can see we are here.' — 'let the whole house know. If it's going to look at us, we should all be visible.' — Athena writing the reply — Hercules's expression at 'everyone': he immediately understands and approves — the plan forming: sunset, west light, full team visible on the Olympus terrace — Zeus's crown straight through all of this: this is a moment he is certain about — #FFD700 and the decisiveness of the main hall scene, Zeus's crown detail the key visual element

scenes.title: The Reply and the Preparation
scenes.narration: The reply went out via dolphin: 'YES. TONIGHT AT SUNSET, LOOK WEST. WE WILL BE ON THE TERRACE. WE WILL KNOW YOU ARE LOOKING.' The team prepared. Not formally — there was no occasion format for this. But people appeared on the terrace through the afternoon in their own ways. Achilles was there early, standing at the coordination post as usual, but stayed. Artemis arrived and chose a position at the terrace edge, which had the clearest sightline. Perseus was there already — he had been there since the message came. Poseidon had his log. Athena had her OBSERVATIONS notebook (she would record this separately from the official correspondence). And then the others, filtering in through the afternoon, until the terrace was full.
scenes.visual_prompt: The reply going out: 'YES. TONIGHT AT SUNSET. WE WILL BE ON THE TERRACE. WE WILL KNOW YOU ARE LOOKING.' — the afternoon preparation: each team member arriving in their own way — Achilles at the coordination post, staying — Artemis choosing the clearest sightline position with purpose — Perseus already there — Poseidon with his log — Athena with her OBSERVATIONS notebook — Hercules arriving, choosing to stand near the center — the terrace filling through the afternoon, each person in their natural position — the sunset approaching, the west light beginning to warm — Archie on the terrace railing, having been there the whole time — the Titan visible in the sea from the terrace, its shape against the eastern horizon — #FFD700 and afternoon terrace light, the gathering quality

scenes.title: The Left-Side Addition
scenes.narration: At mid-afternoon, an unexpected letter arrived from the eastern valley. SEVEN, in caps: 'WE HEARD ABOUT TONIGHT. MEDUSA SAYS WE CANNOT COME. BUT WE WOULD LIKE TO BE COUNTED AS PRESENT. — SEVEN (AND THREE AND FOUR AND FIVE AND TWO AND ONE AND RIVER).' Poseidon read it to the terrace. There was a quiet. Then Hercules said: 'We can say they are here.' 'They sent a letter saying they wanted to be present,' Zeus said. 'That counts.' He produced a small piece of paper. He wrote their names on it. He put it on the terrace railing. 'SEVEN, THREE, FOUR, FIVE, TWO, ONE, RIVER,' he said. 'Present.' Artemis looked at the list. She nodded once.
scenes.visual_prompt: The eastern letter arriving mid-afternoon: SEVEN in all caps requesting to be counted as present — all seven names listed — Poseidon reading it to the assembled terrace — Hercules: 'we can say they are here' — Zeus producing his small piece of paper, writing the seven names, placing it on the terrace railing — reading them aloud: 'SEVEN, THREE, FOUR, FIVE, TWO, ONE, RIVER. Present.' — Artemis's single nod of agreement — the paper on the railing as the eastern correspondence being held present in the west — Zeus's crown slightly left as he places the paper — the terrace now including everyone: the house, and the correspondence, and the formal paper with the snake names — #FFD700 and the terrace railing, the small paper the key visual

scenes.title: Hades Sends a Note
scenes.narration: A second message arrived — this one from the underworld, in the black envelope. It was brief. Hades had written: 'I am aware of tonight's event. The underworld wishes the Titan a clear view. — H. P.S. The left head also wishes this.' Poseidon read it. He looked at the black envelope. He put it on the railing next to the paper with the snake names. 'Hades is present,' he said. 'And the left head.' The paper with the snake names and the black underworld envelope on the railing — the extended correspondence, present on the terrace. Poseidon wrote in his log: EVERYONE IS HERE. He added a sardine. He did not question this. Everyone was here.
scenes.visual_prompt: Hades's note arriving in the black envelope — 'I am aware of tonight's event. The underworld wishes the Titan a clear view. The left head also wishes this.' — Poseidon placing the black envelope on the railing beside the snake names paper — 'Hades is present. And the left head.' — the terrace railing: the snake names paper and the underworld envelope — Poseidon writing in his log: EVERYONE IS HERE — adding a sardine without questioning it — EVERYONE IS HERE: the correspondence fully assembled on the railing — the extended correspondence as physical presence — sunset approaching — the Titan visible to the east — #FFD700 and the railing with both items, the sunset light beginning to warm the terrace

scenes.title: Sunset
scenes.narration: At sunset, the west light came across Olympus exactly as Zeus had described. The terrace caught it fully — the stone of the columns, the team visible, the railing with the paper and the envelope. Poseidon sent a message to the Titan: 'WE ARE HERE.' The Titan looked west. They could see it — slowly, deliberately, the Titan's head turning toward the setting sun, toward Olympus. They watched the Titan watch them. It lasted several minutes. Then Poseidon received a single message via dolphin: 'I SEE YOU.' Zeus's crown was straight for 8 seconds — equaling the series record from EP027. Then it listed left. Then he said: 'Tell it we see it too.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Sunset on the Olympus terrace — the west light landing across the stone columns, the team visible, the railing with paper and envelope — Poseidon's message: 'WE ARE HERE.' — the Titan in the eastern sea, turning its head westward toward the sunset, toward Olympus — the team watching the Titan watch them — the several minutes of mutual seeing — the dolphin relay: 'I SEE YOU.' — Zeus's crown straight for 8 seconds: equaling EP027's series record — then listing left — 'tell it we see it too.' — the full terrace, all correspondence present, the Titan looking at Olympus for the first time since it went underground — the sunset light gold across everyone — #FFD700 and golden sunset, the terrace the stage, the Titan's silhouette the eastern counterpoint

scenes.title: Archie at Sunset
scenes.narration: The team stayed on the terrace after the sun went down. Not formally — there was nothing formal to stay for. They just stayed. Archie was on the railing beside the snake names paper and the underworld envelope. When it was dark, and the Titan's shape was visible only as a silhouette against the night sea, Archie spread his wings once — fully, briefly — and folded them back. He did not fly. He just opened them and closed them, once, toward the sea. Athena saw it. She wrote in the OBSERVATIONS notebook: 'At dark. Archie spread his wings once toward the Titan and folded them again. I don't know what this means. I believe it is a greeting.'
scenes.visual_prompt: The terrace after sunset — the team staying without a reason to stay — dark coming — Archie on the railing beside the paper and envelope — the Titan as a silhouette against the night sea — Archie spreading his wings fully, briefly, toward the sea — folding them — not flying, just the gesture — Athena seeing it — writing in the OBSERVATIONS notebook: 'Archie spread his wings once toward the Titan and folded them again. I don't know what this means. I believe it is a greeting.' — the terrace in the dark, the team's shapes visible, the Titan's silhouette, Archie on the railing — the notebook entry by lamplight — #1A1A4E and night sea, Archie's wing-spread the closing image

viral_hook: The Titan asked permission to look at Olympus. It said: 'I know I cannot come close. Just see it from where I am. Is this possible.' Zeus said yes immediately, set a time for sunset, and told everyone to be on the terrace so the Titan could see they were there. At sunset: 'I SEE YOU.' Zeus's crown straight for 8 seconds. He said: 'Tell it we see it too.'
highlight_note: The Titan looking at Olympus in the sunset. 'I SEE YOU.' Zeus's crown straight for 8 seconds — equaling the series record. Then listing left. 'Tell it we see it too.'
short_title: The Titan Asked Permission to Look. Zeus Said Yes. They Watched Each Other at Sunset.
rewatch_bait: Scene 4 — Zeus writing the snake names on his small paper and reading them aloud. He keeps small papers for things that matter. The snake names on the terrace railing during the Titan's first look at Olympus.
youtube_title: The Titan asked if it could just look at Olympus from where it was. The team got everyone on the terrace. At sunset: 'I SEE YOU.' | Little Olympus S2 EP18
lesson: Permission matters even when it isn't required. The Titan could see Olympus any time it turned its head. It asked anyway, because it wanted us to know it was looking, and it wanted to know we'd let it. Consent is about more than capability — it's about whether the other person knows and agrees.
arc_notes: Titan arc major emotional peak — first seeing Olympus since going underground. Zeus crown straight 8 seconds: ties EP027 series record. Archie's wing-spread: Athena calls it a greeting, doesn't know what it means — intentional ambiguity. Full correspondence assembled on the terrace: snake names paper and Hades envelope on the railing. Poseidon's log: EVERYONE IS HERE with sardine. One more episode (EP039-040 = two-part finale) to complete Season 2.
